entry.title: DEMO: SAP HANA Cloud - Analyzing Employee Churn with Data Science
entry.content:
SAP HANA Cloud is a database platform that can utilize in-memory storage to address business challenges. By combining the advantages of a multi-tiered data architecture with multi-model and data science capabilities, this database management system can simplify complex data silos into a single database that powers multiple solutions. Such an optimized design can then enable the acceleration of the application, user experience, and business processes - creating transformative innovation.

In this demonstration, the issue of employee turnover is addressed using the database's machine learning capabilities. View the accompanying video to learn how a data scientist or data engineer can utilize SAP HANA Cloud to answer critical business questions regarding this type of problem.

 

Recommended Resources:

Blog Post: Machine Learning with SAP HANA Cloud

Documentation: SAP HANA Cloud Machine Learning Overview

Hands-on: SAP HANA Cloud basic trial

entry.title: Re: Activation of scopeitem 1SG for group reporting
entry.content:
Dear @s0016807163,

Question 1.

Scope item 1SG is handled in SAP Central Business Configuration (CBC) ->Scope activities

 

In SAP current CBC guidance, license-dependent scenarios are marked with a lock icon and are not activated only by the implementation team in scoping. For those scenarios, SAP requires a support case with subject Activation of New Licensed Scenarios in component XX-S4C-OPR-SRV. After SAP enables the licensed scenario the implementation team can include 1SG in scope and proceed with deployment

 

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/doc/c38e987da8cf471f8888d703938f2ef1/SHIP3.0/en-US/SAPCentralBusinessConfiguration_ProjectExperience_en-US.pdf

BUT before that, try clicking Edit then the three dots and clearing all filters in scenarios, along with the rest of the settings as in the screenshot and see if the Add button is active

 

Question 2.

1SG should not be treated as an initial-scoping-only scenario by default. SAP explicitly states that additional scope can be added later by using Changes via Edit, including scope extensions after the initial scoping has already been completed. SAP also clearly distinguishes the scenarios that are restricted to initial scoping only. The CBC guide gives examples such as group ledger scenarios and scenario 5R0 and it explains that these can be identified with the Selection Allowed filter. Since SAP calls out those exceptions explicitly, the correct position is that 1SG can be added later as a scope extension, provided the licensed scenario has first been enabled by SAP

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Web Dispatcher configuration for Google Load Balancer
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

 

We have this setup : Google Internal Load Balancer -­> Web Dispatcher (2x) -­> S/4HANA

 

Some users are executing long running Fiori reports by connecting Load balancer (requires 25 minutes to complete).
After exactly 10 minutes, they get an error DP_SOFTCANCEL_GW_CANCEL_BY_RFC_PEER.

 

The exact same reports complete with success when executed using Web Dispatcher (bypassing Load Balancer).

 

I found this information from Google, it seems to be a Google parameter that cannot be changed  (https://docs.cloud.google.com/load-balancing/docs/l7-internal#timeout-keepalive-backends😞
Backend HTTP keepalive timeout
- The backend keepalive timeout is fixed at 10 minutes (600 seconds) and cannot be changed. This helps ensure that the load balancer maintains idle connections for at least 10 minutes. After this period, the load balancer can send termination packets to the backend at any time.
- The load balancer's backend keepalive timeout must be less than the keepalive timeout used by software running on your backends. This avoids a race condition where the operating system of your backends might close TCP connections with a TCP reset (RST). Because the backend keepalive timeout for the load balancer isn't configurable, you must configure your backend software so that its HTTP keepalive (TCP idle) timeout value is greater than 600 seconds.
- When the backend HTTP keepalive timeout expires, either the GFE or the Envoy proxy sends a TCP FIN to the backend VM to gracefully close the connection.

 

Google recommendation is to set the backend (Web Dispatcher) Keep Alive parameter to a value higher than 600 seconds, such as 620.

This is nonsense to me and I don't think it will solve the error : 

- Parameter "icm/keep_alive_timeout", change value from 60 to 620
- Parameter "icm/server_port[]", change value from "PORT=44381,PROT=HTTPS,TIMEOUT=60,PROCTIMEOUT=600,VCLIENT=1" to "PORT=44381,PROT=HTTPS,TIMEOUT=60,PROCTIMEOUT=1800,VCLIENT=1"

 

I would like to have recommendations about this setup please ?

 

Best regards,

Hugo

entry.title: AI and UX sessions at SAP Sapphire 2026 that I’m most excited about
entry.content:
SAP Sapphire is where SAP makes its big announcements about its vision for the future of ERP and business applications. This includes showing new products, presenting customers who have transformed their business using SAP’s solutions, and highlighting new technologies that will shape the future.

For 2026, AI will continue to dominate the event. AI will change the way that people consume enterprise software through chat-based interfaces and screens that generate on-the-fly based on what you want to do. Eventually.

In this post, I’ll share the sessions at Sapphire and ASUG Annual Conference that I’m most excited about. For the full menu, you can browse the session catalog. My preferences are, of course, shaped by my role on the Cloud ERP UX Foundation team. I’ll organize by date so you can plan your agenda. You can also use the browser to search by topic or speaker if that makes it easier for you. Some are even available on-demand. Let’s go!

Monday, May 11, 2026

Day / Time / Session ID

Session title and description

Speakers

Monday
10am – 5pm
ASUG102

Unlocking Business Value with AI: Strategy, Security, and the Road Ahead

Gain a comprehensive and practical overview of SAP Business AI. This workshop will examine core strategies, security frameworks, and future trends while exploring real-world customer stories. The session will conclude with an expert panel and a hands-on AI road mapping session.

Many experts from SAP

Monday
2:00pm – 2:20pm
ERP2143

The future of enterprise: Innovations in SAP Cloud ERP Private

Discover how SAP Cloud ERP Private solutions enable the next era of enterprise transformation. This introductory session explores how cutting-edge artificial intelligence, trusted data, and integrated applications work in harmony, creating smarter, faster, and more resilient businesses within the security and flexibility of your private cloud.

Nicole Smythe, SAP

Vinay V, SAP

Monday
2:30pm – 2:50pm
CXP2149

Legacy to legendary: The AI-enhanced path

Explore the strategic road map for moving from legacy on-premises systems to a cloud-native, AI-enhanced architecture. Discover how a clean core approach helps reduce technical debt, accelerate innovation, and position your organization to use AI for greater efficiency and agility.

 

Monday
3:30pm – 3:50pm
PUX1349

Innovations and value that SAP Business AI offers

Learn how you can find the best AI features for your use cases, and understand the business value and associated cost with our self-service tools.

Pascal Mauge, SAP

 

Tuesday, May 12, 2026

Session title and description

Speakers

Starting with the “My Home” page and drilling down into typical SAP Fiori apps, see how AI makes it easier to find information, enter data, automate tasks, and make informed business decisions. Explore how conversational interactions make SAP solutions more intuitive.

 

Peter Spielvogel, SAP

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio - naming convention for tasks in German...
entry.content:
Hi there,

Unfortunately, the naming conventions apply globally across the entire workspace regardless of what language a process is modeled in.
A few options to work around this:

Option 1: Separate workspaces per language
The cleanest solution. Each workspace can have its own convention rules, so your German workspace keeps "Object+Infinitive" and your English workspace uses "Verb+Object." The tradeoff is that content is separated.
Option 2: Disable or soften the task naming rule
If automated enforcement isn't critical for your English processes, you can remove or set the rule to advisory only, 
and handle naming consistency through manual review instead.
Option 3: Use folder-based process organization with a single relaxed rule
If your German and English processes are organized in separate folders, you could adjust the convention to be less strict globally and rely on team discipline for the language-specific formatting.
Option 4: Submit a feature request
Per-language convention rules is a legitimate gap for multilingual organizations. I'd recommend submitting this on the SAP Influence portal here. 

In addition, you can also look into the Workspace Overview guide available here. Or you can submit a ticket via the SAP Help Portal.

Hope this helps!

entry.title: Re: Not able to create Purchase organization for newly cr...
entry.content:
Hi!

To proceed with the Purchasing Organization creation in the Organizational Structure, ensure that the workspace is in the “Scope and Organizational Structure” phase and that the activity “Set Up Organizational Structure” is opened in Edit mode.

The creation can be performed either in Table View or Chart View. In Table View, the option can be accessed via the search field by entering “+ Create Purchasing Organization”.

In Chart View, it may be necessary to scroll horizontally until the tile “Create Purchasing Organization” is visible.

After accessing the creation option, maintain the required fields, including the Purchasing Organization ID, Name, and the assignment to a Company Code, and then save the changes.

The Purchasing Organization will then be available in the Organizational Structure.

This behavior is standard and related to how the UI presents the available objects depending on the selected view.

Please check KBA: 3735104 - How to create a Purchasing Organization in Set Up Organizational Structure in SAP Central Business Configuration - SAP for Me

Additional information:

  • Organizational Structure Management | SAP Help Portal
  • Organizational Units | SAP Help Portal
  • Purchasing Organization | SAP Help Portal

Best Regards,

entry.title: Re: SAP Signavio
entry.content:
Hi, thanks for raising this question. 

What you're describing with the inconsistent behavior (some users being prompted for credentials, others getting a "page not available" error) is typically related to one of the following:                                             

1. Share link expiration or permissions
The guest commenting links generated in SAP Signavio Process Manager can expire or become invalidated if the process is moved, renamed, or if workspace permissions are updated. Try regenerating a fresh share link for the process and resending it to see if that resolves it as a first step.                  

2. Browser or cookie issues on the recipient's end
Some users hitting a login prompt may have a cached session or cookie conflict. Ask affected users to try opening the link in a private/incognito browser window.

3. Workspace security settings
Check your workspace settings under Admin to confirm that external/guest access for commenting is still enabled. These settings can sometimes be changed during system updates or by another admin.

4. SAP Signavio platform changes
There have been periodic changes to how guest access works in SAP Signavio, particularly as features transition toward the Business Transformation Suite and Process Manager functionality evolves. If this started happening after a recent update, it may be worth raising a support ticket with SAP directly referencing the specific error messages so they can confirm whether a platform-side change is causing it.


Since your processes are still in Process Manager and not yet visible in the Hub, I'd recommend opening a ticket with SAP Support and including the exact error messages users are seeing. That will give them enough to diagnose whether it's a configuration issue or a known bug. You can find info on how to do so here.

Hope that helps!

entry.title: Announcing the First-Ever System-Based SAP Customer Data Cloud Certification
entry.content:
On behalf of the Product Learning team at SAP, I am excited to introduce a new way to validate your SAP Customer Data  Cloud  skills: a system-based certification that measures your ability to perform real tasks and scenarios in SAP Customer Data Cloud. Instead of selecting answers on a theory-only exam, you’ll complete hands-on activities in an SAP-provisioned environment—demonstrating the day-to-day capabilities that matter on live sites. Full details and the latest policies are available on the certification page:

SAP Certified - Implementation Consultant - SAP Customer Data Cloud

 

🌟A New Era of Practical, System-Based Assessment

Unlike standard multiple-choice exams, this new certification provides candidates with:
  • A SAP Customer Data Cloud system, and
  • A PDF containing a set of realistic tasks to complete
Candidates perform actual configuration and scenario-based exercises directly in the system. Once all required tasks are completed, they can submit their results for automated evaluation. Within minutes, candidates will know whether they have passed or failed — offering a faster, more authentic, and more meaningful way to validate capability.
This practical approach ensures that certified consultants are not only knowledgeable but also skilled in applying SAP Customer Data Cloud functionality in realistic customer scenarios.

🎯Why System Based Certifications?

The reimagined SAP Certification exams offer a range of benefits. More specifically:

  • Job-ready proof: Demonstrates you can navigate SAP Customer Data Cloud and execute business-critical tasks—not just recall concepts.
  • Strong SAP signal: An SAP-recognised credential and digital badge that hiring managers and project leads across the ecosystem trust.
  • Practice over memorisation: Preparation aligns to SAP Learning Journeys and hands-on exercises for better retention and impact.
  • Real-world relevance: Validates cross-functional workflows (catalogue, content, search, promotions) that move conversion and revenue KPIs.
  • Always current: Content and recertification align with SAP’s cloud release cycle, reflecting the latest features and UI. 
  • Higher integrity than Q&A-only: Performance on practical tasks reduces guesswork and emphasises authentic ability.

👤 Who Is This Certification For?

This certification is designed for:

  • Consultants

It is classified as an intermediate-level certification and requires approximately 3 hours to complete.

📚How to Prepare

To set yourself up for success, we recommend the following preparation path:

1. Complete the "Implementing SAP Customer Data Cloud" Learning Journey

The Learning Journey for Consultants on learning.sap.com provides the foundational knowledge required for the hands-on portion of the certification. They combine structured courses with access to practice systems for guided exercises, building familiarity with the interfaces, workflows, and configuration steps assessed.

2. (Optional) Attend the In-Person Classroom Trainings

For learners who prefer guided instruction, tan intructional led training is available:

  • SAP Customer Data Cloud Implementation

This class provides expert-led, structured instruction and practice to deepen your understanding and skills.

 

📢Spread the Word!

Help us share this exciting update with partners, colleagues, and other stakeholders.

📝We Value Your Feedback

After completing the certification, please take a moment to share your feedback. Your insights help us continuously improve the learning experience.
 
Two easy ways to share feedback!
  1. Click “Share Feedback” to submit your thoughts. 

     

  2. In the “Notification Center” under “Learning” you can select the congratulatory email after completing course or learning journey and click “Feedback” to share your thoughts.

entry.title: Is this possible to cancel Billing Document automatically In SAP Public Cloud

entry.title: Clarification on cds.queued and same-db microservices when upgrading to CDS9
entry.content:
Hi,

We are upgrading a CAP Node.js project involving microservices sharing the same database container from CDS8 to CDS9 and we are having some issues with the Queueing with cds.queued feature. Specifically, since the transactional outbox which now is activated by default stores messages in the cds.outbox.Messages, we get the different microservices "polluting" each other with events belonging somewhere else (which often crashes the container completely due to hard errors such as required services not existing in the other container).

We are looking for clarification on:

  • We expect that setting "requires": { "queue": { "kind": "in-memory-queue" } }" (as shown in the documentation) brings it back to the default behavior in CDS8 (i.e. it is still delayed emission, but it happens without a persistent outbox meaning we may fail to emit the message).
  • Setting "requires": { "queue": { "kind": "in-memory-queue" } }" does not actually work. We needed to use "requires": { "kinds": { "queue": "in-memory-queue" } }" to make that work. That seems to be a bug in the default configuration (in-memory-queue refers back to the default queue configuration and inherits its kind -> i.e. it circles back to persistent-queue anyway). Is this known and being addressed, or are we maybe doing something wrong?
  • Is there a suggested pattern for using outboxes with same-db-microservices? The documentation for CAP Java indicates that this is a known limitation and suggests a manual approach, we do not see anything like that on the nodejs side though? Is that planned? Is there any proposed workaround (looking in the source code I do not see any environment variables or similar which would allow to e.g. use a dedicated outbox table per service)?
  • We have a lot of event driven data replication patterns which go like the bullets below without outboxes. If the event emission of Service B fails, the processing of the incoming event fails (despite the transaction actually having committed) and we can track that in the DMQ on the way into Service B. We rely on this to achieve "at least once" SLA. If we introduce an outbox we assume this no longer happens, instead it is the outbox which needs to be monitored and retried. Is this correct (i.e. if we start using such outboxes we must also start managing the dead-letter-queue to achieve the same SLA)?
    • Service A commits an update, then emits to eventmesh
    • Service B replicates the changed data from service A, does some processing on its own, commits that, then emits its own eventmesh message
    • Service C picks up the message and processes.

Any advice much appreciated, thanks in advance!

//Carl

entry.title: Re: What I Wish I Knew
entry.content:
hi all, 

I wish I’d known earlier…

How important the technical foundations really are — understanding APIs, integrations, and how tools like Signavio connect within the SAP ecosystem.

I also wish I’d known upfront how critical it is to set up Signavio with governance in mind from day one, rather than treating it as just a modelling tool.

And finally, that the biggest challenges aren’t technical at all — they’re about process ownership, process governance, and building a mature process community, especially when there are many overlapping and conflicting capabilities across an organisation.

The technology is powerful, but sustainable value really comes from aligning it with people, roles, and clear decision-making structures.

entry.title: Re: Personas 3.0 : How to determine if it's called from P...
entry.content: OK, so there is a way to find out whether the session runs in webgui or Slipstream. Function module ALEWEB_GET_CONTEXT with field name ~RG_WEBGUI will tell in the first row of the resulting internal table if the session is running via the personas or webgui service. Field name ~RG_PMOBILE with value X means that it's a Slipstream session. However, I haven't found any way to find out if a flavor is currently active, unless it is passed via a URL parameter - in which case that is visible by using field name sap-personas-flavor. But that is not the typical way because normally the flavor comes from assignment via Personas administration.

entry.title: Re: Situation Handling for Journal Entry Posting – Trigge...
entry.content:
Hello,

It appears that you would like to implement a custom Situation Handling scenario and trigger it after a Journal Entry is created. Unfortunately, this is not supported for SAP S/4HANA Cloud customers at the moment.

Please consider the following restrictions:

1. Class CL_FINS_JOURNALENTRY_SOT_EVENT can’t be used as a trigger class. It is an incorrect usage.

2. Custom Situation Handling requires customer‑developed objects.

3. Triggering Situation handing invokes a workflow event. However, the relevant interfaces and classes for this workflow integration are not released for customer consumption, meaning they cannot be called directly in customer code.

Hazel

entry.title: No SLOC is specified in SO, confirmed qty equals 0 when No MRP AREA has storage?
entry.content:
As the factory has MRP area:

When the SO is created, the storage location(SLOC) cannot be determined, so there is no storage location on the SO line.  However, when the material in the MRP area has no stock, the SO does not have a confirmed qty, which causes the corresponding DN cannot to be created.

For example：

Factory 1000 MRP areas: SLOC 1001 and 1002, with no stock of material A;

Factory 1000 Non-MRP areas: SLOC 2001 and 2002, with enough stock of material A.

When placing the order, the SO’s storage location is empty. Even if location 2001 has stock, the confirm qty of the SO still equals 0, resulting in the inability to create a DN.

Can this issue be resolved through standard configuration? Like When no SLOC is specified in SO, the ATP check for the SO confirmed QTY is not limited to the MRP area, but to check the whole Plant storage. Thanks a lot!  Hope for your reply.

 

 

 

SAP S/4HANA for advanced ATP

entry.title: Posting Logic of FIBTI: How SAP Finance Transactions Are Orchestrated
entry.content:
SAP Finance is a collection of several applications — In-House Banking,  Payment Management, and more. Some of them can run remotely on different system instances and need to integrate with the main FI system. Also, non-Finance applications like Payroll or business transactions in the context of Real Estate need such remote integration with an FI system. This raises a fundamental challenge: how do you ensure every business transaction, regardless of where it originates, gets posted to Accounting correctly and consistently? This is the value of Finance Business Transaction Interface (FIBTI) and Accounting Business Transaction Interface (ABTI). FIBTI acts as the single entry point for all incoming business transactions — performing checks, deriving key fields like the posting date, and storing each transaction as a finance notification. ABTI then takes over as the bridge to Accounting, transforming that data into balanced journal entries while handling all accounting logic — G/L account determination, valuation, and derivations — so that sender applications never need to. If anything goes wrong, nothing is lost: FIBTI's built-in reprocessing capability, surfaced through the Manage Finance Notifications app, lets operators monitor and recover failed postings across all scenarios.

How FIBTI's Posting Logic Works

When a business transaction arrives at FIBTI, it follows a clear sequence:

  1. Receive and store the transaction as a FinanceNotification business object
  2. Derive central fields — most notably the posting date.
    Note:
    Some business transactions already contain a posting date whereas others only contain some date or timestamp fields that are available in the sender application where the business transaction originated. In the latter case the posting date is determined within FIBTI and is saved in the FinanceNotification business object.
    Posting date determination in general is considered accounting logic, not the sender application's responsibility.
  3. Call Accounting Business Transaction Interface (ABTI) to transform the transaction data into journal entries.
  4. Enable reprocessing via the Manage Finance Notifications app if anything fails.

  ABTI handles the heavy lifting in two steps:

  1. Build a raw journal entry from the incoming transaction data
  2. Enhance it through checks, derivations, and G/L account determination to produce the final journal entry.

Posting Logic by Scenario

In-House Banking (IHB)

FIBTI receives an IHB account balance change FinNotification and derives the posting date from the BankAccountPostingUTCDateTime timestamp. ABTI then transforms the IHB account balance change finNotification into a balanced pair of journal entry line items: 

  - One line item posted to a Receivables/Liabilities account

  - One offsetting line item to a clearing or expense account.

This notification covers any transaction that changes the balance of an inhouse bank account in the IHB application — including payments, interest postings, and bank fees. The G/L account is determined by the IHB Scenario (for example, Intercompany Payment, Payment on Behalf) and is configurable via the FJEPVC_IHB_ACC_DET view cluster. For SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, this configuration activity is Account Determination for Inhouse Banking. (ID: 105994) . The profit center is derived from the bank area, and the assignment reference is carried over from the IHB account transaction to enable automatic clearing later.

 Payroll

Payroll data arrives at FIBTI highly aggregated to protect employee privacy. Each payroll item is transformed by ABTI into one of two journal entry line item types:

  • Expense (Category = E) → debit expense G/L account on a cost center
  • Liability (Category = L) → credit payroll liability G/L account, with amount sign-reversed to balance the journal entry to zero

The posting date comes from the proposed posting date in the notification, but can be overridden in the Manage Finance Notifications app during reprocessing. If line items exceed 999, ABTI automatically splits the journal entry. The Assignment Reference (Payroll Payment Reference) on liability lines is critical for automatic clearing with the corresponding payment.

Payment Transaction Events

Every step in the payment lifecycle triggers a payment transaction event notification to FIBTI. Each event posts from one clearing account to the next, making payment progress visible in Accounting at every stage.

Each event has a predecessor and successor subnode, which ABTI transforms into a balanced pair of journal entry line items. The posting date is derived differently depending on the event direction:

  • Inbound events (payment already executed) → BankAccountPostingUTCDateTime
  • Outbound events (payment not yet executed) → ReqdBankExecutionUTCDateTime

A consistent assignment reference across consecutive events ensures the automatic clearing program can match and clear the related line items on shared clearing accounts.

 End-to-End Example: Payroll Payment Process

The payroll and payment transaction event scenarios work together in the payroll payment process:

  1. Payment creation → The payment platform notifies FIBTI → posts from the payment inbound suspense account to the payment suspense account.
  2. Payment-to-Payroll mapping → Connects the payment to the payroll liability G/L account by crediting the payment inbound suspense account and debiting the payroll liability G/L account.
  3. Payroll posting → Debits expense G/L accounts on cost centers and credits the same payroll liability G/L account.

Shared assignment references across all three steps tie the postings together and enable automatic clearing — creating a complete, auditable chain from salary expense to bank payment.

Key Takeaways

  • FIBTI's own posting logic is minimal — its primary role is orchestration and posting date derivation.
  • ABTI handles the actual transformation into journal entries, in two steps: raw journal entry build, then enhancement.
  • Assignment Reference is the backbone of automatic clearing across all scenarios.
  • The Manage Finance Notifications app is the operational tool for monitoring and reprocessing failed transactions.

Related Documents

For more information about FIBTI and ABTI, please visit the topics on the SAP Help Portal:

  • Finance Business Transaction Interface (FIBTI) 
  • Manage Finance Notifications 
  • Accounting Business Transaction Interface (ABTI)

entry.title: Re: Asset Accounting
entry.content:
Hello @sai_kumar1 ,

Follow below approach.

 

TopicExplanation
Root cause of issueWhen using AS91/AS92, if takeover values (APC + accumulated depreciation) are not aligned with depreciation key, useful life, and takeover date, SAP cannot spread depreciation correctly and posts it as a lump sum.
Why depreciation is not periodicThe system thinks remaining useful life is zero or inconsistent, so it posts full remaining depreciation in the next period instead of distributing it.
Correct approach (Recommended)✔ Do NOT manually recreate assets via AS91/AS92 for this scenario. ✔ Use Asset Transfer (ABUMN) or Asset-to-Asset transfer (ABT1N) to move balances from old asset to new asset class.
Why transfer is better✔ Transfers APC + accumulated depreciation + remaining useful life correctly ✔ Keeps depreciation continuity intact ✔ No recalculation issues
If AS91/AS92 must be usedEnsure: ✔ Correct capitalization date (original) ✔ Correct useful life (remaining life) ✔ Correct depreciation key ✔ Set takeover date properly
Key field to checkRemaining Useful Life → must not be zero Otherwise system posts full depreciation immediately
After correctionRun depreciation recalculation: ✔ AFAR (Recalculate depreciation)

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: Moving Average Price Not Updated and Zero Valuation a...
entry.content:
The root cause of your issue is likely incomplete integration between valuation classes, material ledger settings, and scope item 7NH. Simply creating new valuation classes and G/L accounts is not sufficient—additional configuration steps are required to ensure that moving average price updates and goods receipt valuation flow correctly into FI. Dependencies with inventory valuation and event-based production costing also play a role.

Having said that, perform the below steps

1) Check OBYC Settings:
Verify that all new valuation classes are mapped to the correct G/L accounts.

2) Run Cost Estimates:
For materials without prior valuation, release a standard cost estimate to enable GR valuation.

3) Confirm Scope Item Dependencies:
Ensure 1GA (Inventory Valuation) and 33O (Event-Based Production Costing) are active and integrated with 7NH.

4) Material Ledger Closing:
Execute CKMLCP at period-end to update moving average prices.

entry.title: Re: Is this possible to cancel Billing Document automatic...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, automatic cancellation of E‑Way Bills, e‑Invoices, and billing documents are not supported. The cancellation process must be triggered manually or via SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) APIs. While certain extensibility options (BAdIs) exist for customizing e‑document processing, there is no standard BAdI that cancels billing documents and their linked e‑documents automatically.
E‑Way Bill cancellation is subject to strict timelines (within 24 hours in India). Automation must respect these compliance rules. Unlike on‑premise, you cannot enhance core cancellation transactions directly in Public Cloud; only extensibility points (BAdIs, APIs) are allowed.

entry.title: Re: Need guidance on whether the COGS account in S/4HANA...
entry.content: Yes, in SAP S/4HANA 2023, your source COGS account (e.g., 500001) can remain a non‑cost element, while the target COGS split accounts must be defined as cost elements. The split logic posts from the source account into the detailed cost component accounts, which are then used in Margin Analysis. This design aligns with best practice for COGS splitting in S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Controle the issuance for consumption w.r.t prod. ord...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud, the standard reservation created from a production order will indeed list all required components at once. However, the system does not natively provide a “sequential issue” control at the reservation level, it assumes that all components are available and can be issued when needed. That said, there are ways to meet your business requirement.

Options to control sequential issuance

1. Partial Goods Issue Against Reservation
Store department can perform goods issue in multiple partial postings against the same reservation. For example, issue 10 components today, and the remaining 10 tomorrow. Each partial issue is logged, so delays or staggered issuance can be tracked in the material document history. This is the simplest standard approach.

2. Backflush vs. Manual Issue
If you use manual goods issue (instead of backflush), production can request only the subset of components needed for the day. This gives control to production and stores to align issuance with space constraints.

3. Reservation Change / Split
In Public Cloud, you cannot directly split reservations into multiple smaller ones as like in on‑premise. But you can adjust quantities in the reservation or create additional reservations for staggered issuance. This requires manual intervention but provides visibility.

4. Custom Extensibility (BAdI / Custom Logic)
You can use In‑App Extensibility (Custom Logic) to add rules. For example, when a production order is created, automatically split the reservation into multiple “lots” based on business rules (e.g., 10 components per day). Or enforce a workflow where stores can only issue a defined subset each day. This would require a small enhancement but is feasible in Public Cloud.

5. Analytics for Delay Tracking
Use Material Documents app or Production Order Confirmation reports to track when components were actually issued. This gives visibility into delays and supports space‑constraint monitoring.

entry.title: Re: procedure for creating the TM 121/543 return/consignm...
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA, the behavior you are seeing is expected. The supplier field (LIFNR) in MIGO is not editable during subsequent debit postings (A11), because the system always references the purchase order vendor. That is why your attempt to change it via BAdI fails with Message No. MIGO050 – Field LIFNR not ready to accept input.

Options to handle your requirement

1) Use Partner Functions in PO:
Maintain the Goods Supplier partner in the PO (vendor 10173065). This way, reporting and logistics can reflect the delivery supplier, even though FI postings remain with the PO vendor.

2) Custom Enhancement (Extensibility):
In Public Cloud, direct modification of LIFNR in MIGO is not possible. You could use In‑App Extensibility (Custom Logic / BAdIs) to trigger additional postings or create a follow‑on document referencing the delivery supplier. However, you cannot override the locked vendor field in MIGO itself.

3) Alternative Process Design:
If the business requires FI postings to hit the delivery supplier, you may need to model this as a consignment process or third‑party PO, where the delivery vendor is the one referenced in the PO itself. This avoids the mismatch between PO vendor and delivery vendor.

entry.title: Re: S4Hana 2023 On Premise - ATP run in VA02 doesn't see...
entry.content:
To answer both your questions where SAP checks requirements vs. available stock in VA02 ATP, below is the explanation

1) Trigger:
When you run ATP in VA02, the system calls FM AVAILABILITY_CHECK_CONTROLLER, which delegates to AVAILABILITY_CHECK_S4.

2) Requirement Source:
Requirements are rebuilt from VBEP (schedule lines) of the sales order. These are then passed into the ATP engine; VBBE is updated later, but not used directly for the check.

3) Supply Source:
The ATP engine (CL_ATP_TIMESERIES) constructs a time series of supply elements (stock from MARD, planned receipts, purchase orders, production orders).

4) Comparison Logic:
The class CL_ATP_PAC orchestrates the matching of demand (from VBEP) against supply (time series). The core comparison is done inside CL_ATP_BASIC_METHOD_CONTROLLER, which balances requirement quantities against available quantities in the time series buckets.

Please note VBBE is not the comparison table. It is updated after the ATP check to reflect the requirement situation. The ATP engine does not read VBBE to decide availability; it regenerates requirements from VBEP and compares them against the time series. That is why manual changes to VBBE don’t influence ATP re‑checks and the system ignores them and rebuilds requirements dynamically.

entry.title: Re: CDS : How to define Hierarchy View
entry.content:
Hi Lina,

Excellent blog! This is exactly the kind of structured explanation that's hard to find in one place — thank you for putting it together.

I'm running into an error that I believe is directly related to the hierarchy setup you've described, and I'd love your input on it.

System Version: S/4HANA On-Premise 2025

Error in Eclipse ADT (Problems Tab): 
"Annotations of NodeId use hierarchies, but NodeId does not have any [Analytics]" - In my Analytical Projection view. 

My Setup:

  1. I have a Analytical CDS projection view with annotations where a field is marked as Hierarchy on my NodeId field. 
  2. I'm on 2025 on-prem, so "define hierarchy" syntax should be fully supported
  3. I have the source view defined, but I'm unsure if the association to the hierarchy view itself (as you described) is correctly wired
  4. Additionally, even after the hierarchy is defined, my Analytical Query is not recognizing the hierarchy on the annotated field

Hierarchy Construction Approach Flat Data with "UNION ALL":
Rather than having a pre-existing parent-child table, I am building the hierarchy on the fly from a flat data structure using `UNION ALL`. The hierarchy has 4 levels, and each level is constructed as a separate branch in the UNION ALL, simulating the parent-child relationship programmatically within the CDS source view itself. For example:
Level 1 → Root node (e.g., Project)
Level 2 → Child of root (e.g., WBS)
Level 3 → Child of Level 2 (e.g., Cost Element Grp)
Level 4 → Leaf node (e.g., Cost Element)

Each UNION ALL branch assigns the appropriate `NodeId`, `ParentId`, `HierarchyLevel`, and `NodeType` fields to stitch the levels together into a parent-child structure.

My understanding from your blog:
The source view must carry:
1. A parent-child association
2. An association back to the hierarchy view

I suspect my `NodeId` annotated field is not backed by a properly associated `define hierarchy` view — which is why ADT is flagging it, and possibly also why the Analytical Query fails to pick it up.

My questions:

  1. Is this error typically caused by a missing or incorrectly wired association between the source view and the `define hierarchy` view?
  2. Does the `define hierarchy` approach support a source view built entirely via `UNION ALL` across 4 levels, or does it expect a native parent-child table?
  3. Do I need `@Analytics.dataCategory: #DIMENSION` on the source view for the hierarchy to be recognized correctly in the Analytical Query - Since my projection is on a '#CUBE'.
  4. Is there any additional configuration needed for the Analytical Query to expose and recognize a hierarchy defined via `define hierarchy` on a specific field — especially in 2025 on-prem?

Any pointers or a sample snippet showing the full chain (flat UNION ALL source view → hierarchy view → directory view → analytical query) for a multi-level hierarchy would be incredibly helpful!

Best Regards,
Sabarish

entry.title: Request to Preserve AppGyver’s Legacy: Consider Open‑Sourcing the Pre‑SAP Codebase
entry.content:
I've been a forever fan of Appgyver, sorry in SAP terms SAP Build Apps. Frankly, I saw it with some sadness that the offering is now in deprecation. However, it wasn't really unexpected. The decline of dedicated no‑code tools in the age of LLM‑assisted code generation is understandable. The speed and volume of code agentic coding can deliver does reduce the perceived commercial need for highly visual builders. However, AppGyver’s value was never limited to “just” being a tool; it was a gentle on‑ramp for non‑experts, a playground for experimentation, and a platform that required relatively little investment to become a long‑lasting, sustainable project.

Beyond the technology, there was also a clear sense of intellect and ethos in the original AppGyver team. The openness to community feedback, a commitment to empowering non‑developers, and a design‑first mindset that honestly feels harder to find in large enterprise environments. Seeing that spirit fade into the more rigid structures of a major corporate ecosystem is, frankly, a cultural loss as much as a technical one.

I fully understand that fully open‑sourcing an SAP‑owned product such as SAP Build Apps (the successor of AppGyver) is unlikely to align with SAP’s policies or strategic direction. However, I would like to respectfully propose a more modest and symbolic ask: consider open‑sourcing the AppGyver codebase as it existed prior to the SAP acquisition.

Naturally, following the acquisition the product evolved tremendously, and became an integral part of SAP. I still am convinced, however, the pre‑SAP state of AppGyver still represents an important milestone in professional no‑code development. Releasing that codebase (even if clearly marked as historical artifact) would: 

  • Preserve the intellectual and technical legacy of a pioneering no‑code platform.

  • Allow the community, researchers, and educators to study and learn from its architecture.

  • Enable interested individuals or small groups to invest time and effort into maintaining or evolving it independently, if they choose.

  • Signal that SAP values the early no‑code community and its contributions, even if the formal product line is being retired.

The release of the pre‑SAP code as open source would feel like a more humane and forward‑looking gesture from SAP, rather than just another deprecation, especially given the unexpected nature of this one. It would clearly not bind SAP to continued maintenance, but it would more likely honor the spirit of the original AppGyver project and give its knowledge a second life in the wider developer ecosystem.

I appreciate the work SAP has put in the low/no-code space over the past years, and thank you for taking the time to consider this request.

entry.title: Re: Compatibility Pack: How to check it in your system going forward?
entry.content:
@SamuliK Yes, absolutely.

It is ultimately customer's business decision to conclude whether they use the item in their business or not. But for CP, the check is client dependent. So, there would be no cross-client false positives for CP items (please note it is relevant only for new CP relevance check).  All the rules for checking are carefully curated by experts to determine relevancy. So, the chances of getting false positives with CP check is really minimal or only if the table in very specific system and client has still old uncleared entries. Customer needs to investigate such false positives thoroughly.

BR,

Shwethamani M

entry.title: Re: Tax Code Valid Date Change
entry.content:
I recommend the Best Practice

  1. Always create a new tax code for each VAT rate change.
  2. Keep both tax codes active during the transition period.
  3. Align ELSTER mapping so both rates are reported separately.
  4. Test the VAT return form in the system to confirm correct grouping before submission.

entry.title: Over-valuation of Inventory
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm using SAP S4 HANA public cloud. When posting a Goods receipt, the quantity is being rounded from 4 decimal places to 3 decimal places when converting from pounds to tons. As a result, the Inventory and GR/IR GLs are recording a higher value than actuals.

While posting the corresponding supplier invoice, which has the correct $ value of inventory received, the differential between GR and the invoice is being recorded as a gain in GL Account 52531000 – Gain Valuation of External Products. I understand that the the right way to record the differential is to reduce inventory by crediting the differential amount to Inventory GL and debiting GR/IR account when posting the supplier invoice. How do I rectify this at the Supplier invoice posting stage?

entry.title: How to Set Up SAP Sustainability Data Exchange
entry.content:
Why Setup and Integration Matter

To enable effective data exchange across the supply chain, systems must be properly connected. SAP Sustainability Data Exchange relies on strong integrations to ensure that product carbon footprint data flows securely and consistently between partners and platforms.

A well-configured setup enables:

  • Accurate and automated data exchange
  • Reliable integration across sustainability solutions
  • Scalable collaboration with business partners
  • Consistent and trustworthy sustainability reporting

What You’ll Learn in This Learning Journey

This short, 16-minute course focuses on the essential integration setup for SAP Sustainability Data Exchange:

  1. Integration Capabilities and Setup
    Learn how to configure technical integrations between SAP Sustainability Data Exchange and key systems, including:
  • SAP Sustainability Footprint Management
  • Catena-X 

This ensures seamless data exchange and accurate sustainability reporting across your ecosystem.

Who Should Take the Course?

This learning journey is designed for:

  • Consultants supporting sustainability and integration scenarios
  • Administrators responsible for system setup and connectivity

Prerequisite: Basic knowledge from the Learning Journey Getting Started with SAP Sustainability Data Exchange.

Enable seamless sustainability data exchange and start the Learning Journey “Setting Up SAP Sustainability Data Exchange” here.

entry.title: Bringing AI Directly Into SAPUI5 Controls - AI UI5 Library
entry.content:
A lot of AI work in the SAP space focuses on agents, assistants, and orchestration. This post is about something smaller in scope: bringing AI directly into SAPUI5 as reusable controls, close to where users interact with data every day.

Users frequently work with text fields, writing descriptions, filling in notes, composing messages. Tasks like improving the tone of a text, fixing spelling mistakes, or translating a field value are small, well-defined, and a good fit for what LLMs do well. This post shares an experiment in packaging that kind of capability into a UI5 control library.

The idea: AI as reusable UI5 controls

The library (be.wl.ai) provides a set of AI-powered UI5 controls that can be dropped into any XML view. A consuming app developer doesn’t need to know anything about AI Core or set up any AI infrastructure, they reference the library, add a control and bind it to a model property:

<TextArea value="{/description}" />

<ai:AIPolishButton text="Polish" value="{/description}" />

The TextArea and the AI button are both bound to the same model property. When the user accepts the AI result, it flows back into the model and the TextArea updates automatically, standard UI5 two-way data binding, nothing custom required.

What’s in the library

The library provides 11 controls covering the most common AI text operations:

https://github.com/lemaiwo/ui5-ai-features

Text transformation (Accept/Reject dialog):

  • AIPolishButton — improves grammar, clarity, and professionalism
  • AIGenerateButton — generates text from a short description
  • AISpellCheckButton — corrects errors and shows a summary of changes
  • AIToneButton — rewrites text in a different tone (formal, casual, persuasive, etc.)
  • AITranslateButton — translates into 12 languages via a dropdown menu

Analysis (read-only results):

  • AISummarizeButton — produces a concise summary
  • AIExtractKeywordsButton — extracts keywords as tokens with copy-to-clipboard
  • AICompareTexts — compares two texts and shows differences in HTML
  • AISentimentIndicator — shows a positive/neutral/negative sentiment badge

Inline assistance:

  • AIInputSuggestion — opens a popover with an AI-suggested improvement
  • AIAutoComplete — completes partial text inline

Every control uses standard UI5 two-way data binding through a value property. Bind it to the same model property as your input field, and the AI result flows back automatically.

Architecture: simple on purpose

The controls connect to a CAP backend service that wraps SAP AI Core’s Orchestration Service. The library creates a single shared OData model internally, app developers never see it.

The key design decision: the controls own their connection to the AI service. The consuming app doesn’t need to configure a model, set up a destination or know anything about AI Core. Import the library, use the controls, done.

Under the hood, all button controls extend a shared AIBaseButton base class that handles the OData call, dialog lifecycle, busy indicators, and accept/reject flow. Building a new AI control takes about 50 lines of code, just override the prompt, icon, and event name.

Design decisions worth noting

A few choices shaped how this library ended up, and they’re worth explaining for anyone considering a similar approach.

The controls own their AI connection. Rather than requiring the consuming app to configure a model or destination, the library manages its own OData connection to the CAP backend internally. This keeps the integration surface small, an app developer just binds a value property, the same way they would with any other UI5 control.

Standard UI5 patterns throughout. The controls use properties, events, and data binding exactly as UI5 developers would expect. There’s no custom state management or framework-specific logic. This makes the controls easier to understand, debug, and extend.

Prompts are exposed as properties. Every control has a prompt property that overrides the default. This makes it straightforward to adapt a control’s behaviour for a specific domain or use case without forking the library.

No SAP-specific beta features. Everything here runs on generally available SAP technology, UI5, CAP, and AI Core Orchestration Service. There’s no dependency on preview APIs or experimental tooling.

Current status

This is a first version and a proof of concept of what’s possible. It works locally with cds watch and the controls are fully functional. The library is structured for deployment to SAP BTP (MTA with HTML5 repo host), but it hasn’t been tested deployed yet.

The code is intentionally kept simple. There’s no streaming, no conversation history, no RAG. Just a text in, text out pattern, which covers a surprising number of real use cases.

Try it yourself

Prerequisites

  • Node.js 20+
  • An SAP AI Core instance (extended plan) with access to the Orchestration Service
  • A deployment of a model like GPT-4o through SAP AI Core

Running locally

The AI controls call SAP AI Core’s Orchestration Service on the backend. To run this locally, you need to use the hybrid approach — your CAP server runs on your machine but binds to a real AI Core service instance on SAP BTP.

  1. Clone the repository:
git clone https://github.com/lemaiwo/ui5-ai-features.git
cd ui5-ai-features 
npm install
  1. Log in to your Cloud Foundry environment:
cf login
  1. Bind to your AI Core instance using the hybrid profile:
npm run bind
  1. Start the dev server with the hybrid profile:
npm run watch-hybrid
  1. Open the app at the URL shown in the terminal:

http://localhost:4004/be.wl.ai.aidemo/index.html

Using the library in your own app

The library lives in app/ai-lib and the demo app in app/aidemo. To use the controls in your own app, add the library as a dependency and start using them in your XML views:

<mvc:View xmlns:ai="be.wl.ai">
    <TextArea value="{/notes}" />
    <ai:AIPolishButton text="Polish" value="{/notes}" />
    <ai:AITranslateButton text="Translate" value="{/notes}" />
    <ai:AISpellCheckButton text="Spell Check" value="{/notes}" />
</mvc:View>

The controls use two-way data binding. Bind both the TextArea and the AI control to the same model property, when the user accepts the AI result, the TextArea updates automatically.

Takeaways

This started as an experiment to see how much AI functionality could be packaged into reusable UI5 controls without requiring app developers to understand the AI layer. The answer is: quite a lot, with a relatively small amount of code.

The text in, text out pattern is simpler than agent-based approaches, but it fits a wide range of real use cases that users encounter daily. It also sits comfortably alongside agent and MCP work, both approaches address different parts of the problem.

The code is available if you want to explore, fork, or adapt it. Feedback and ideas are welcome.

Result

entry.title: Bank Statements with no payment transaction type group
entry.content:
Hi all,

Does anyone know which settings determine the interpretation algorithms used when the bank statement doesn't have a payment transaction type group?

We upload one of our bank statements via an Excel file using Manage Bank Statements. It gets uploaded without anything being entered in the field Payment Transaction Type Group.

With this particular bank statement I can see that items are being allocated using interpretation algorithm 001, but I am not sure where to change this setting. I know that where there is a payment transaction type group it would normally be done under Make Global Settings for Electronic Bank Statement. But I'm not sure how to change it in this case.

We are using S4Hana Cloud Public.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Bank Statements with no payment transaction type group
entry.content:
Good day @kezelda

Thank you for your question.

If your uploaded Excel bank statement shows no Payment Transaction Type Group, the interpretation algorithm is still normally driven by the bank account transaction type group plus the external transaction in SSCUI 101024, not by that blank display field alone. So, to change algorithm 001, first check the bank account’s Payment Transaction Type Group in Manage Bank Accounts, then change the external transaction type in assignment in Make Global Settings for Electronic Bank Statement.

Here are references for more guidance

3432166 - External transaction code & is not specified for transaction type - SAP for Me

3517704 - No transaction type assigned to bank account & & - S/4HANA Public Cloud - SAP for Me

3374309 - Posting and processing rules in bank statement posting - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - SAP for Me

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/ee9ee0ca4c3942068ea584d2f929b5b1/5f07121204f54500a783e7fe6425c4c5.html?version=2602.00&locale=en-US

Before you make changes in SSCUI 101024, first confirm the transaction type source. To do so:

  • Open Manage Bank Accounts, select your relevant bank account,

  • go to Bank Relationship, Check Payment Transaction Type Group,

  • if it is blank, then maintain the correct value, for example the format relevant for that account such as MT90 or CAMT053.

Finally, in Manage Bank Statements, upload your Excel file again and check the bank statement item.

 

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: XCO_CP_XLSX Currency/Quantity Formatting
entry.content:
Hi @Juwin ,

Yes, it is possible, please take a look at the document.

Below is code example (implemented on S/4 Public Cloud with developer extensibility), method set_value_transformation is allowing to apply the conversion. You can create your own conversion class by implement interfaces IF_XCO_XLSX_WA_VT_CELL_VALUE, IF_XCO_XLSX_WA_VT_ROW_VALUE or using SAP provided one via xco_cp_xlsx_write_access=>value_transformation->best_effort.

" fill excel data from 2nd row
    DATA(lo_pattern_data) = xco_cp_xlsx_selection=>pattern_builder->simple_from_to( )->from_row(
                                xco_cp_xlsx=>coordinate->for_numeric_value( 2 ) )->get_pattern( ).
    lo_sheet->select( lo_pattern_data
      )->row_stream(
      )->operation->write_from( REF #( lt_excel_data )
      )->set_value_transformation( xco_cp_xlsx_write_access=>value_transformation->best_effort
      )->execute( ).

lt_excel_data have structure like below:

define structure zo2c_oil_attachment {

  customer_invoice      : vbeln;
  orderline             : posnr;
  sales_document        : vbeln;
  art_number            : matnr;
  art_description       : maktx;
  bu                    : spart;
  document_currency     : waerk;
  batch                 : charg_d;
  coo                   : land1;
  customs_code          : abap.char(30);
  customer_date         : erdat;
  tax_code              : mwskz;
  barcode               : ean11;
  invoice_inbound       : abap.char(50);
  @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure : 'zo2c_oil_attachment.deliveryquantityunit'
  quant                 : abap.quan(13,3);
  deliveryquantityunit  : msehi;
  transfer_price        : abap.dec(31,2);
  price                 : abap.dec(31,2);
  value                 : abap.dec(31,2);
  calculatedcurrency    : abap.char(6);
  @Semantics.quantity.unitOfMeasure : 'zo2c_oil_attachment.weightunit'
  weight_netto_per_unit : brgew;
  weight_netto          : abap.dec(31,2);
  weightunit            : msehi;

}

Hope this help!

Linh

entry.title: Fiori My Inbox attachment deletion issue and SWI1 Log inconsistency using SAP_WAPI_ATTACHMENT_ADD
entry.content:
Workaround 1: Force a Context Save via SAP_WAPI_WRITE_CONTAINER Right after adding the attachment, I call SAP_WAPI_WRITE_CONTAINER with an empty container. This successfully triggers the Transaction_Manager->Save(), updates the Workitem timestamp, and Fiori My Inbox allows deletion perfectly.

The Drawback: In transaction SWI1 (Workitem Log), the Executed Action displays the technical name SAP_WAPI_WRITE_CONTAINER right below Attachment Added. This looks unprofessional to business users and auditors.

Workaround 2: Bypass RAP LUW via DESTINATION 'NONE' Calling SAP_WAPI_ATTACHMENT_ADD with do_commit = 'X' in a separate LUW ( DESTINATION 'NONE').

The Drawback: This breaks the transactional consistency of RAP. If the RAP LUW fails during the Save Sequence, the attachment is already committed, leading to inconsistent data
Is there a RAP-compliant and standard way to add an attacthment ?

Looking forward to more such informative posts!

Best regards

entry.title: Improvement Request: Hide Inactive Templates in 360 Executive Review (SuccessFactors)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I’m looking for insights from other SuccessFactors customers who use 360 Executive Review. We’ve noticed an issue that becomes more visible as organizations create new 360 templates over time.

The challenge

In the 360 Executive Review, inactive templates still appear in the template filter list. As more templates are created and retired, the list becomes cluttered, making it harder for managers, HRBPs, and administrators to quickly find the correct template. This also increases the risk of selecting outdated ones.

Suggested enhancement

We’ve submitted an SAP Influence idea proposing one of the following improvements:

  • Hide inactive templates from the main filter
  • Add filter options such as:
    • Show All Templates
    • Show Active Templates Only
  • Or allow administrators to control template visibility via RBP

Why this matters

Improving this would help:

  • Reduce process errors
  • Improve usability for managers and HRBPs
  • Strengthen governance and compliance
  • Streamline administrative work

If your organization uses 360 Reviews and faces similar challenges, I’d really appreciate your support.
You can view and vote for the idea here:
👉 Improvement Request Details - Customer Influence

Happy to hear your experiences or any alternative approaches you’ve used.

Thanks in advance!

Alina

entry.title: Introducing UI5 Plugins for Claude: AI-Powered Development for SAPUI5 and OpenUI5
entry.content:
Introducing UI5 Plugins for Claude: AI-Powered Development for SAPUI5 and OpenUI5

We're introducing UI5 plugins for Claude, an open-source collection of plugins for Claude Code. These plugins are designed to enhance UI5 development workflows with AI assistance.

What Are Claude Code Plugins?

Claude Code is Anthropic's AI-powered CLI for software development. Its plugin system lets domain experts extend Claude's built-in capabilities with specialized knowledge, tools, and skills for a specific technology. With plugins, Claude Code becomes aware of framework-specific conventions, APIs, and best practices. This means developers get more accurate, context-aware assistance without having to explain the fundamentals every time.

Why We Built This

The UI5 framework has a rich but specialized programming model. Developers, especially those new to UI5 or returning after a break, often spend significant time looking up the correct patterns for class definitions, module loading, TypeScript typing, and toolchain configuration. AI assistance can save hours in these situations, but only if the AI has accurate, curated knowledge of the framework.

Rather than relying on the LLM's general training data, which can be outdated or incomplete for framework details, these plugins embed current, maintained guidelines directly into the development workflow. The result is that Claude Code provides UI5-accurate suggestions from Day One, so you don't have to paste documentation into every conversation.

Last year, we published the UI5 MCP server described in Give Your AI Agent Some Tools: Introducing the UI5 MCP Server. The introduction of agent skills over the last year makes some tasks easier to complete compared to using only the UI5 MCP server. MCP servers aren't going away; this just introduces a new way to distribute tasks between MCP servers or skills. The choice depends on the task: Skills work well for tasks described in free text, while MCP servers offer better support for tasks that require precise input and output.

What We Are Releasing

The initial release ships two plugins:

1. UI5 Plugin — Your UI5 Development Companion

The UI5 plugin equips Claude Code with deep knowledge of the SAPUI5 and OpenUI5 ecosystem. It helps you with the following tasks:

  • Create new UI5 projects: Create scaffolded project structures based on best practices without any manual setup.
  • Detect and fix UI5-specific errors: Claude understands UI5 linting rules and applies fixes based on the official guidelines.
  • Access UI5 API documentation: Get accurate, framework-aware answers about controls, modules, events, and APIs instead of generic JavaScript suggestions.

Technically, the plugin is a wrapper around the UI5 MCP server. Install it with a single command:

claude plugin install ui5@claude-plugins-official

Or from within Claude Code:

/plugin install ui5@claude-plugins-official

2. UI5 TypeScript Conversion plugin — Migrate to TypeScript with Confidence

Migrating a JavaScript UI5 project to TypeScript can be tricky. Generic AI tools often handle the required transformations incorrectly. These transformations are necessary for the UI5 class system, the sap.ui.define module loader, runtime-generated getter and setter methods on controls, and library-specific patterns. The UI5 TypeScript Conversion plugin provides Claude Code with a step-by-step conversion playbook.

Install it with:

claude plugin install ui5-typescript-conversion@claude-plugins-official

Get Started

  1. Install Claude Code.
  2. Install one or both plugins as shown above.
  3. Start coding. For example, you can ask Claude Code to create a UI5 project, run UI5 linter, explain an API, or start a TypeScript migration.

The source code for both plugins is available on GitHub at UI5/plugins-claude under the Apache 2.0 license. We welcome contributions, bug reports, and feature requests via GitHub Issues.

You can also join the conversation in the #ai channel of the OpenUI5 Community Slack or ask questions on Stack Overflow using the ui5-plugins-claude tag.

entry.title: Queue Determination & Activity Areas for Internal Replenishment/Stock Transfers
entry.content:
Introduction:

Internal replenishment is an important process in SAP Extended Warehouse Management. Stock needs to be moved from bulk storage to pick face bin storage in an efficient way. However, the right resource needs to pick up the job at the right time, which is one of the common issues.

When I began to look into Queue Determination and Activity Areas to resolve this, I found that there was documentation on the topic within SAP, but there was nothing that took you through the process step by step. There was nothing within the SAP community that took all of this together.

So, I have done this configuration end-to-end in the sandbox system. This blog is all about learning how to configure Queue Determination and Activity Areas in the context of internal replenishment in Embedded EWM in S/4HANA 2025. The scenario is as follows: replenishment tasks should be assigned to the right queue so that only specific resources, in this case, forklift resources, are busy with those tasks and other processes are not disrupted

Whether you’re setting this up for the first time or trying to figure out why tasks are not routing correctly, such as bins not having Activity Areas assigned or WPTs not being marked as Queue Relevant, this guide will take you through the basics, the resources, and finally the testing.

 

Configuration Steps:

  1. Define Storage Type, Storage Bin, Activity Area Configurations and Master Data
    • Define Storage Type

Path: SPRO → IMG → SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Master Data → Define Storage Type

Define the source storage type, which's the bulk zone and the destination storage type, which is the pick face zone. If they are not already set up, you need to put them in place. The source storage type should be zone. The destination storage type should be picking face zone.

  • Define Activity Area

Path: SPRO → IMG → SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Master Data → Define Activity Areas

  • Define Storage Bin Structure

Path: SPRO → IMG → SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Master Data → Define Storage Bin Structure

  • Assign Storage Bins to Activity Areas

Path: SPRO → IMG → SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Master Data → Assign Storage Bins to Activity Areas

  • Define Sort Sequence for Activity Area

Path: SPRO → IMG → SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Master Data → Define Sort Sequence for Activity Area

  • Do Bin Sorting -- /N/SCWM/SBST


  1. Resource and Queue Management Configuration and Master Data
    • Define Resource Types

Path: SPRO → IMG → SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Cross-Process Settings → Resource Management → Define Resource Types

  • Define Queue Types

Path: SPRO → IMG → SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Cross-Process Settings → Resource Management → Define Queue Types

  • Define Queues

Path: SPRO → IMG → SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Cross-Process Settings → Resource Management → Define Queues

  • Define Queue Determination Criteria

Path: SPRO → IMG → SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Cross-Process Settings → Resource Management → Define Queue Determination Criteria

This is where you connect your Activity Area and Warehouse Process Type to a queue. The system checks these rules from top to bottom based on their priority level. It uses Activity Area and Warehouse Process Type to decide which queue to use. The order of priority matters.

  • Maintain Resource Group -- /SCWM/RGRP

  • Maintain Queue Sequence for Resource Group -- /SCWM/QSEQ

Need to assign the replenishment queue to your resource group. Then you must define the priority of the replenishment queue. The priority is important because if you have a resource group that is assigned to queues the system will look at the priority to figure out which queue to take tasks from first. This means the system will offer tasks from the replenishment queue with priority.

  • Maintain Resource -- /SCWM/RSRC

Need to open each forklift resource and assign the forklift resource to the replenishment queue. Make sure the Active flag is turned on for the  forklift resource. This is an important step that people often forget to do. If you do not do this the user will see a task screen even when the replenishment queue is full of tasks, for the forklift resource.

  • Maintain Users -- /SCWM/USER

Assign your user to the resource you created in step 2.7. This links the user login to the warehouse resource and its queue. So your user and warehouse resource are connected.

  1. Define Warehouse Process Type and Removal Strategy Configurations. 
    • Define Warehouse Process Type

Path: SPRO → IMG → SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Cross-Process Settings → Warehouse Process Types → Define Warehouse Process Types

When you are doing your WPT replenishment make sure the box that says Queue Relevant is checked. If you do not check this box the warehouse tasks will be. They will not be put into a queue for the WPT replenishment.

Also configure:

Define WPT

Define PTDI

Determine WPT

  • Specify Stock Removal Rule

Define the rule that controls how stock is picked from the source bins during restocking. The rule should specify which stock to choose from the source bins. It helps to maintain inventory levels. The rule makes sure that the right products are restocked. Source bins are used to store products before they are restocked. The rule is used during the replenishment process. Replenishment is when stock levels are brought back up to normal. Stock is selected from source bins based on this rule. It is a part of inventory management. The replenishment process relies on this rule. Source bins provide the stock that is needed. The rule helps to prevent stockouts and overstocking. Accurate stock selection is crucial, during replenishment.

  • Specify Storage Type Search Sequence

The system searches for storage types in an order when it needs to pick stock, for replenishment. It looks at the kinds of storage where the stock is kept. The system checks one type of storage after another to find the stock. It does this in a sequence to make sure it finds the stock it needs to replenish. The system searches the storage types in this order when selecting stock for replenishment.

  • Define Stock Removal Control Indicator

Controls additional stock removal behavior such as partial quantity handling.

  • Determine Storage Type Search Sequence for Stock Removal

Link the Storage Type Search Sequence to the replenishment process.

  1. Warehouse Order Creation Rule Configuration and Master Data
    • Define Number Ranges for Warehouse Order

Path: SPRO → IMG → SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Cross-Process Settings → Warehouse Order → Define Number Ranges for Warehouse Order

  • Assign Warehouse Number to Number Range Interval

  • Define Sort Rules for Warehouse Tasks

Define Sort Rule:

SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> SCM Extended Warehouse Management -> Extended Warehouse Management -> Cross-Process Settings -> Warehouse Order -> Define Sort Rules for Warehouse Tasks

Define Sort Fields:

  • Define Limit Values for the Size of a Warehouse Order

Control the maximum number of warehouse tasks or weight per warehouse order. This prevents users from receiving oversized work packets during replenishment runs

  • Define Creation Rule for Warehouse Orders

Create the Warehouse Order Creation Rule (WOCR) for replenishment. Link the sort rule, limit values, and queue assignment defined in the earlier steps.

  • Define Search Sequence of Creation Rules for Activity Areas

Testing

  • Trigger a replenishment warehouse order via /SCWM/REPL

  • Go to Warehouse Monitor -- /SCWM/MON

  • Execute /N/SCWM/REPL

  • Need to check the log

  • Press Next Task the replenishment task should appear on screen

  • Confirm the task and verify stock movement in the Warehouse Monitor

Conclusion:

Queue determination for replenishment in Embedded EWM is really complicated. It has four parts that need to work

Here are the common problems:

  • Bins are not assigned to Activity Areas
  •  The Work Pool Template or WPT is not marked as Queue Relevant
  • The resource is not set to Active in the queue assignment

When all four parts of Queue Determination for replenishment are set up correctly the system automatically sends replenishment tasks to the correct resource. This makes your warehouse run smoothly and it is easier to manage Queue Determination for replenishment. Queue Determination for replenishment in Embedded EWM is very important. I hope this helps you with Queue Determination for replenishment. Please leave a comment about Queue Determination for replenishment. Share with others about Queue Determination, for replenishment.

entry.title: Mastering Resource Management in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Behind every smooth warehouse operation in SAP EWM, resource management plays a huge role.
It ensures work is assigned intelligently, executed efficiently, and aligned with real business priorities.

Here, I want to show you something different - how the system actually thinks. How does it make decisions? How it behaves under the hood when resources are being managed. We're not starting from the surface; we're starting from the bottom and building our way up.

By the end of this, you won't just know what to configure - you'll understand why the system behaves the way it does.

Managing warehouse resources in high-volume automotive spare parts distribution centers requires tight synchronization between configuration and execution.

Here’s a quick cheat sheet on key T-Codes and the business logic behind them:

Key Configuration Codes:

Queue Determination → /SCWM/QSEQ

Warehouse Tasks are auto-sorted into specialized work streams by WPT, Activity Area & Storage Type.

→ Engines ≠ Small Parts. The system knows that before the operator does.

Resource Definition → /SCWM/RSRC

Every forklift, picker, and reach truck is defined here, with its resource type and capacity constraints.

→ No resource = no assignment. Garbage in, garbage out.

Resource Group Assignment → /SCWM/RGRP

Links resources to eligible queues. Only qualified operators get assigned to the right task class.

→ You don't send a cherry picker to handle a pallet of axles.

 

Queue Priority Sequence → /SCWM/QSEQ

Controls task urgency logic. Production supply & urgent dealer orders surface to the top — automatically.

→ No manual intervention. Priority is governed, not guessed.

Task Interleaving → /SCWM/QTSQ

The real efficiency multiplier. Combines Put away → Picking → Replenishment in one optimized travel loop.

→ Eliminates deadheading (empty travel). Operators stay productive across the full shift.

 RF Execution → /SCWM/RFUI

Guided, Poka-Yoke scanning at the point of execution. Zero tolerance for mispicks or wrong put-away.

→ The system validates. The operator confirms. Accuracy is non-negotiable.

 

The Workflow (Real Scenario):

The Workflow (Real Scenario):

In Warehouse 3900, inbound Small Automotive Parts (fast-moving SKUs) are received, and Warehouse Tasks are created based on source and destination data.

  • The system assigns Queues based on activity area, storage type, and process type [V_T346L → WP 1010 | PTWY → INBOUND]
  • Now the system looks for a Warehouse Order. The WOCR assigns it to an Activity Area — that Activity Area, mapped in QSEQ, triggers the queue assignment to the resource [/SCWM/QSEQ → HUMA | Seq 1 → INBOUND]
  • When operator MOHD logs into RFUI, the Resource Group logic dynamically assigns tasks [/SCWM/RSRC → MOHD | RT01 | HUMA | YE00]
  • High-priority tasks (e.g., production supply or urgent dealer orders) are pushed first [QSEQ Seq 1 governs the order, not the supervisor]
  • Task Interleaving ensures operators perform Put away → Picking → Replenishment in one optimized travel loop [/SCWM/QTSQ → HUMA | Seq 1 → OUTB]
  • Operator completes the Task and logs off the resource.

Result: Reduced empty travel, faster order fulfillment, and improved labor utilization

Conclusion:

Resource management in SAP EWM isn't a configuration exercise; it's a design decision. Queues reflect real work streams. Resource groups reflect real skill sets. Task interleaving reflects real movement on the floor. When you understand that, you stop configuring the system and start designing it. In automotive distribution, the stakes are real. A delayed Automotive parts kit doesn't sit in a bin. it stops a production line. Get the foundation right, and the warehouse runs itself. That's not an exaggeration; that's just good configuration in practice.
